page: Microsoft Azure D16nds_v6 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the Dndv6 family. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel processor and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, maintaining a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per virtual core.

This server type includes 945 GB of local storage and is premium storage capable, allowing for high-throughput disk configurations. As part of a network-optimized family, the instance is designed to handle workloads requiring consistent network throughput alongside local disk access. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included with this instance.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D16nds_v6 offers a balanced resource density of compute, memory, and local storage, making it suitable for general-purpose workloads. The combination of dedicated CPU resources, 64.0 GB of RAM, and local disk capacity supports applications such as relational databases, web serving, and mid-tier application servers that require reliable performance without the need for dedicated GPU acceleration.
description: Microsoft Azure D16nds_v6 is an x86_64 architecture server instance from the network-optimized Dndv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel processor, paired with 64.0 GB of system memory, establishing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core ratio. For storage, the instance is equipped with 945 GB of local disk space and supports premium storage configurations. This server does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The resource profile of the D16nds_v6 provides a balanced density of compute, memory, and local storage, making it suitable for general-purpose applications, relational databases, and web serving workloads that require consistent performance without specialized hardware accelerators.
